name: Route of the Fuente de San Benito – circular from Mugares
name_original: Ruta de la Fuente de San Benito – circular desde Mugares
region_intro: Hiking around Toén offers routes through a landscape characterized by rivers, waterfalls, and thermal areas. The region features varied terrain, including paths along the Río Barbantiño and trails leading to natural springs and waterfalls. Hikers can explore a network of paths that traverse river valleys and gentle hills, providing diverse natural scenery. Best hiking trails around Toén The most popular hiking route is Camino Natural del Río Barbantiño – circular from A Forxa, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete, following the natural course of the Barbantiño River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Fuente de San Benito – circular from Mugares, a moderate 10.4 miles (16.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads to the Fuente de San Benito. Local hikers also love the Barbantes Thermal Park loop from Razamonde, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through the area around Barbantes Thermal Park, often completed in about 3 hours 4 minutes. Hiking around Toén is defined by river paths, thermal springs, and waterfalls, with routes ranging from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 31 easy, 34 moderate, and 3 difficult routes. The routes in Toén are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Toén's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around Toén?
A: Hiking around Toén offers a diverse landscape characterized by rivers, waterfalls, and thermal areas. You'll find paths along the Río Barbantiño, trails leading to natural springs and waterfalls, and routes that traverse river valleys and gentle hills. The terrain varies, providing a rich natural scenery for hikers.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Toén?
A: Toén boasts a comprehensive network of over 60 hiking routes. These include 31 easy, 34 moderate, and 3 difficult trails, ensuring options for various skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Toén?
A: Yes, Toén features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976234765/camino-natural-del-rio-barbantino-circular-from-a-forxa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Río Barbantiño – circular from A Forxa&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the natural course of the Barbantiño River. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979327815/route-of-the-fuente-de-san-benito-circular-from-mugares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Fuente de San Benito – circular from Mugares&lt;/a&gt;, known for its significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Toén?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its waterfalls and natural springs. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24311709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall – Molinos de Pazos de Arriba loop from Punxín&lt;/a&gt;, which specifically highlights a waterfall along its path.

Q: Are there any thermal areas or hot springs accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, Toén is notable for its thermal areas. You can hike routes that lead through or near these natural features, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park loop from Razamonde&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31857136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park loop from Barbantes&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; are a nearby attraction.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Toén?
A: The hiking routes in Toén are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the river paths, and the presence of thermal springs and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Toén?
A: Toén offers 31 easy routes, many of which are suitable for families looking for less strenuous walks. These trails often follow river paths and gentle hills, providing enjoyable outdoor experiences for all ages.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see around Toén?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several natural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; are popular. You might also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3132926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrelo de Miño Nautical Park&lt;/a&gt; for scenic views.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the hiking areas?
A: Yes, the area around Toén is rich in history. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella (Roman Bridge of Ourense)&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Main Square of Ourense&lt;/a&gt;, or see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of San Martín de Ourense&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt; also offer a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Toén?
A: Given the region's characteristics of rivers, waterfalls, and thermal areas, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery, making them ideal for hiking. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially on trails that follow rivers or lead to natural pools.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in Toén?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Toén offers 3 difficult routes. These trails typically feature more significant elevation gains and rugged terrain, providing a more demanding experience for experienced hikers.
tips: Path with excellent views of the Miño River as it passes through the outskirts of the city of Ourense.

A few kilometers from Ourense is the monumental rural church from the 18th century. The temple, originally Romanesque in style, has been restored and transformed over time, incorporating new styles such as Gothic or Baroque.
